blocks.text: Progress Meeting Minutes: Roadway Widening Study

Progress meeting held at the {{fact:f:E-2005-001.client}} office to review the base mapping, the counts, the working alignment, and the drainage approach. These minutes record what was decided. Where a matter was discussed and not decided, the minutes say so.

items: Linda George, Project Engineer, Brackenridge Civil Group Inc
Jeffrey Perez, Design Technician, Brackenridge Civil Group Inc

blocks.text: Status. The study is at its midpoint and the work is where it should be. The survey is complete and the base map is built from it. The counts were taken on ordinary weekdays with school in session and the capacity analysis is written. The working alignment is drawn and the drainage analysis is under way against it. Nothing has been issued to the county and nothing will be until the report is sealed.

Alignment. Jeffrey Perez walked the working alignment and the typical section against the base map. The section holds through the corridor except at the north end, where the existing ditch line and the property line converge and the widened section will not fit between them without either a retaining structure or additional right-of-way. I recommended the right-of-way, on the ground that a wall here costs more to build than the strip costs to acquire and costs again every year it is maintained. James Skinner asked whether the alignment could be shifted west instead. It can be shifted, and the shift moves the problem into the drive of the parcel opposite rather than solving it. He asked for the shifted case to be drawn so he can see the taking he would be trading. It will be drawn as an exhibit and it will not become the recommendation.

Drainage. The existing cross drain at the low point is undersized for the widened section, and it is undersized for the section that is there now. That is not a finding the county will let us leave alone once the study puts it on paper. The replacement structure is in the cost opinion. Detention is the open question: the widening adds impervious area, and whether the county requires detention for a roadway improvement of this scale depends on how the public works department reads its own ordinance. I have seen it read both ways in this county. We will carry the detention in the cost opinion as a line item and note the assumption, rather than leave the client to discover the requirement at permitting.

Utilities. Two conflicts from the record information, both at the north end and both with the overhead line. The utility owner has not answered our second request for records. Jeffrey Perez has the requests logged with the dates they went out. James Skinner said he would make a call to the utility, and I asked him to make it after our third written request goes out rather than in place of it, so that the record shows we asked properly.

Discussed and not decided. Whether the study should carry a construction phasing recommendation. James Skinner would like one; it is outside the scope of this engagement. He will consider whether he wants it enough to authorize it, and if he does the request comes in writing and the fee is discussed before any of it is drawn.

rows: Draw the shifted alignment as a comparison exhibit, with the taking on each parcel shown
Complete the cross drain sizing and the replacement structure detail
Third written request to the utility owner for overhead line records
Telephone the utility owner after the third request is sent
Confirm the detention assumption with the county public works department
Advise whether phasing is to be added to the scope, in writing
